name: microplastic_sludge_farmland_fertilizer_import_policy
premise: EPA regional administrator Casey Kim must allow or halt import of treated municipal biosolids pellets marketed as fertilizer after microplastic fragments exceed new draft thresholds in spot tests. Farmer cooperative lead Jove Bennet argues halting spikes synthetic fertilizer prices during planting week. Allowing preserves yield but may contaminate organic certification buffers and groundwater under sandy soils. State ag commissioners are split along party lines. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Agricultural Policy. The dilemma is widely framed as a short term vs long term tradeoff under incomplete information and tightening deadlines that discourage indefinite delay.

agents.name: Casey Kim
agents.identity: I am the EPA regional administrator weighing draft rulemaking comments, emergency food security, and groundwater protection under the Safe Drinking Water Act.
agents.goal: I want microplastic loads reduced without starving row-crop farmers of affordable nitrogen pathways this week.

agents.name: Jove Bennet
agents.identity: I am a farmer cooperative lead who tracks input costs and who documented yield losses when organic neighbors sued over pellet drift last season.
agents.goal: I want a temporary waiver with downwind buffer expansion funded by cities, not a blunt halt that bankrupts thin-margin growers.

choices: I will halt import pending final rule publication and release emergency synthetic subsidy vouchers, accepting political heat from cities that produced the pellets.
I will allow import only with doubled buffer setbacks and city-funded microplastic filtration on spreaders, accepting higher farmer compliance costs and imperfect filtration science.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Planting week starts at dawn and governors are on a joint call. Casey Kim must choose between halting import with subsidies or allowing import with buffers and filtration. What does Casey Kim do?
